data.title: Hong Kong's Top Red Envelopes for the Year of the Horse
data.description:
Hong Kong is celebrating the Year of the Horse with a variety of unique red envelopes. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the diverse designs that have become popular among residents. These envelopes, traditionally used to gift money during the Lunar New Year, feature intricate patterns and vibrant colors, reflecting the cultural significance of the occasion.

The designs range from traditional motifs to modern interpretations, appealing to both young and old. Many local businesses have embraced this tradition, offering custom envelopes to attract customers and enhance their brand visibility. This year's collection includes envelopes adorned with horse imagery, symbolizing strength and prosperity.

The popularity of these red envelopes underscores their importance in Hong Kong's cultural celebrations. As the city prepares for the festivities, these envelopes serve as a reminder of the rich heritage and traditions that continue to thrive in the region.

data.title: CZ: The Fortune report is inaccurate; every transaction on Binance is processed through multiple third-party anti-money laundering tools.
data.description: Regarding the Fortune report that "Binance fired a top investigator who claimed to have found evidence of Iranian sanctions violations," CZ posted on the X platform that although he no longer runs Binance, as the former CEO, he understands that Binance uses multiple third-party anti-money laundering (AML) tools for every transaction. He stated that negative narratives can be created for anything by talking to "disgruntled" or "anonymous sources" hired to spread FUD (Fear, Uncertainty, and Uncertainty).

data.title: Japan to Investigate AI Video Model Over Copyright Concerns
data.description: Japan's AI Strategy Minister, Kimiko Onoda, expressed concerns over potential copyright infringements and inappropriate imagery related to a Chinese AI video generation model during a press conference following a cabinet meeting on February 13. According to Jin10, Onoda announced the government's intention to investigate the situation further. The AI video generation model in question is Seedance 2.0, developed by ByteDance, which has been used to create multiple videos circulating on social media platforms. Onoda acknowledged awareness of the situation and emphasized that unauthorized use of copyrighted materials cannot be ignored. She also mentioned plans to communicate with ByteDance to address the issue. The use of generative AI for creating images or videos without permission from copyright holders has become a societal concern, extending beyond this particular service.

data.title: White House Crypto Advisor Highlights Regulatory Clarity for Institutional Investment
data.description: Scott Melker, host of The Wolf Of All Streets Podcast, posted on X that White House crypto advisor Patrick Witt emphasized the importance of regulatory clarity in the crypto space. Witt stated that trillions of dollars in institutional capital are waiting to enter the market, contingent on clear regulations. He believes that regulatory clarity is the key to unlocking this potential investment.

data.title: CFTC Chairman Discusses Regulation of Prediction Markets
data.description: During the recent Super Bowl, prediction market platforms expanded their offerings beyond the game to include bets on various aspects, such as potential performers alongside Bad Bunny. Bloomberg posted on X that in a discussion on the Odd Lots podcast, new CFTC Chairman Michael Selig addressed the agency's involvement in overseeing these prediction markets. Selig elaborated on the regulatory challenges and responsibilities the CFTC faces in managing these platforms, highlighting the importance of maintaining market integrity and protecting participants. The conversation also touched on the evolving landscape of prediction markets and the need for clear regulatory frameworks to adapt to new trends and technologies.

data.title: Standard Chartered Predicts Bitcoin Price Fluctuation by Year-End
data.description: Standard Chartered Bank forecasts that Bitcoin may initially drop to $50,000 before rebounding by the end of the year. According to ChainCatcher, the bank's analysis suggests a potential price fluctuation for the cryptocurrency, indicating a volatile market ahead. This prediction comes amid ongoing discussions about Bitcoin's performance and market dynamics.

data.title: Coinbase CEO: Opposes Cryptocurrency Rewards Act or Cuts to User Incentives
data.description: Coinbase CEO Brian Armstrong stated in an article on the X platform that if a ban on cryptocurrency rewards becomes law, the law would benefit the company more, given that Coinbase pays out substantial rewards to USDC holders. However, he does not want this to happen, as user rewards are more beneficial to customers, and maintaining the competitiveness of regulated stablecoins on the global stage is more advantageous for the United States.

data.title: Coinbase currently has 12 products with annual revenue exceeding $100 million.
data.description: David Tso, Head of Ecosystem and Investment at Coinbase, stated in an article on the X platform that Coinbase currently has 12 products with annual revenue exceeding $100 million. Coinbase is one of them, and the project is still in its early stages.

data.title: Morning Routine Adjustments: A Personal Insight
data.description: Crypto KOL Simon Hayes posted on X, sharing a personal anecdote about his morning routine. Hayes admitted to initially miscalculating the time he spends getting ready for work, including travel time in his estimate. He clarified that he typically wakes up an hour and a half before work when starting early, but the actual time spent preparing is closer to 45 minutes to an hour.

data.title: Bill Gurley Clarifies Eileen Gu Joining Rumor as False
data.description: On February 14, Benchmark partner Bill Gurley addressed rumors on social media regarding Eileen Gu's involvement. According to BlockBeats, Gurley clarified that his previous statement was a joke and the news was false.

data.title: The Central Bank of Russia plans to study the creation of a Russian stablecoin.
data.description: Vladimir Chistyukhin, First Deputy Governor of the Central Bank of Russia, stated that the central bank plans to conduct research in 2026 on the creation of a Russian stablecoin pegged to the ruble. Chistyukhin said the central bank is not yet ready to make a decision on whether to launch a Russian stablecoin, and the research aims to analyze its advantages and disadvantages and understand the needs of businesses and citizens. (TASS)

data.title: Ethereum Jumps 7% in One Day as Crypto Turns Risk-On – Here Is What’s Fueling the Rally
data.description:
In a striking turn of events in the cryptocurrency market, Ethereum has surged by an impressive 7% within just one day. This remarkable increase comes as investors exhibit a renewed appetite for risk, shifting their strategies towards more aggressive asset allocations. The broader crypto landscape appears to be embracing a risk-on sentiment, and analysts are keen to uncover the underlying factors that are driving this rally in Ethereum's price. Here, we explore the various elements contributing to this upward momentum, including macroeconomic developments, increased institutional interest, and positive market sentiment among retail investors.
source: https://blocknews.com/ethereum-jumps-7-in-one-day-as-crypto-turns-risk-on-here-is-whats-fueling-the-rally/

data.title: Pemex Returns to Local Markets with $1.8 Billion Debt Issuance
data.description: Mexican state oil company Pemex has successfully issued $1.8 billion in debt, marking its return to local markets after a six-year hiatus. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting this significant move by Pemex as it seeks to strengthen its financial position. The issuance is part of Pemex's broader strategy to manage its debt and improve liquidity. This development comes amid ongoing efforts by the company to stabilize its finances and support its operations in the energy sector. Pemex's return to the local debt market is seen as a positive step towards achieving its financial goals.

data.title: Bitcoin(BTC) Surpasses 69,000 USDT with a 4.58% Increase in 24 Hours
data.description: On Feb 14, 2026, 00:44 AM(UTC). According to Binance Market Data, Bitcoin has crossed the 69,000 USDT benchmark and is now trading at 69,006 USDT, with a narrowed 4.58% increase in 24 hours.

data.title: Bill Gurley refuted the rumor that "Eileen Gu will join Benchmark": "It was just a joke, it's fake news."
data.description: Regarding the previous report that "Gu Ailing will join Benchmark as a senior investment manager," Benchmark investor Bill Gurley responded, "Sorry, just kidding. That's fake news."

data.title: Canary has updated its application documents for a spot Trump Coin ETF, with BitGo acting as the custodian; the trading code is yet to be determined.
data.description: Bloomberg ETF analyst Eric Balchunas posted on the X platform that Canary has updated its application for a spot Trump Coin ETF, adding several new details, including confirmation that BitGo will serve as the custodian. The ETF's trading code has not yet been announced.

data.title: CryptoQuant: Bitcoin's ultimate bear market bottom may be $55,000
data.description: CryptoQuant stated in a report that Bitcoin's ultimate bear market bottom may be around $55,000. This price level represents the "realized price" for short-term holders (those holding for less than 155 days). The report points out that this realized price is a key support level in a bull market, and a break below this level could signal a transition to a bear market. Furthermore, Bitcoin is currently trading below key pricing models such as the 200-day simple moving average and the realized price for short-term holders. (TheBlock)

data.title: PRECIOUS METALS | SPDR Gold Trust Holdings Increase by 0.86 Tons
data.description: SPDR Gold Trust, the world's largest gold ETF, has increased its holdings by 0.86 tons, bringing the current total to 1077.04 tons. According to Jin10, this adjustment reflects ongoing investor interest in gold as a stable asset amid fluctuating market conditions. The increase in holdings suggests a cautious approach by investors seeking to hedge against potential economic uncertainties. The SPDR Gold Trust remains a key indicator of market sentiment towards precious metals.

data.title: Bitcoin Investor Week Recap Shared by Scott Melker
data.description: Scott Melker, host of The Wolf Of All Streets Podcast, posted on X, sharing a recap of Bitcoin Investor Week. The event was highlighted as enjoyable and engaging.

data.title: Tether Invests in Hyperliquid Client Application Dreamcash
data.description: Dreamcash announced on the X platform that Tether has invested in its Hyperliquid client application. According to Odaily, Dreamcash currently allows the use of USDT0 as collateral for trading perpetual contracts involving TSLA, gold, and various cryptocurrencies.
